codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of fullscreen video background landing page redesign. VideoBackground component correctly implements autoplay/loop/muted/playsInline with dark overlay and fallback gradient. HeroSection is clean with proper CTA routing. All required sections render (promo, hero, trust, features, use cases, testimonials, release, pricing, FAQ, footer). Auth-conditional header works correctly. Card-based trust indicators section follows the Cardboard.dev aesthetic. Orange accent used consistently in section dividers and CTAs. TypeScript clean, lint clean on changed files. Tests pass at baseline level.

issues.description: AnimatePresence wrapping motion.video is unnecessary. The video is either rendered or not (based on hasVideoError), but AnimatePresence is designed for exit animations of unmounting children, which isn't being used here. The opacity transition is already handled by motion.video's animate prop. Remove AnimatePresence wrapper for code clarity.

TestimonialsSection uses bg-black which makes the video background invisible in this section. While this matches the pattern of other existing sections (NewReleasePromo, Features, etc.), it was a newly created file where the worker could have opted for a transparent/semi-transparent approach to let the video show through. Not blocking since the visual result is consistent with other sections.

Pre-existing purple color remnants remain in TestimonialCard's featured gradient array (lines 34, 38: 'from-purple-500 to-pink-500', 'from-violet-500 to-purple-500'). The worker fixed the quote mark color (line 66) but missed these gradient definitions used for featured card borders. These are outside the strict feature scope but worth noting as residual purple-to-orange migration items.

Component exports both named (export function HeroSection) and default (export default HeroSection). Landing.tsx imports it as default, while VideoBackground is imported as named. Minor inconsistency in export/import conventions across new files, but functionally correct.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No convention documented in AGENTS.md for whether new components should use named exports, default exports, or both. Worker created files with both patterns (VideoBackground and HeroSection each have both named and default exports), while Landing.tsx mixes import styles (default for HeroSection, named for VideoBackground).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/landing/VideoBackground.tsx:86 (export default), src/components/landing/HeroSection.tsx:152 (export default), Landing.tsx:4 (import HeroSection - default), Landing.tsx:16 (import { VideoBackground } - named)

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly noted in skillFeedback that the frontend-worker skill's 'write tests first' step is not applicable for purely visual/layout redesigns with no new logic. The skill should document when test-first is optional to avoid future workers spending time on inapplicable steps.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The frontend-worker skill suggests writing tests first, but for a purely visual/layout redesign with no new logic or utilities, this step is not applicable.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Multiple landing components still contain purple color references (#8b5cf6, purple-500, violet-500) despite the orange/amber color migration being listed as a precondition. Components affected: MotionBackground.tsx (purple gradients), FeatureHighlight.tsx (text-purple-400), ProductShowcase.tsx (purple gradient hover), WorkflowDiagram.tsx (purple node colors), FAQSection.tsx (#8b5cf6 borders), TestimonialCard.tsx (purple gradient arrays). These were pre-existing and outside this feature's scope, but represent incomplete prior migration work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: grep for '#8b5cf6|purple' in src/components/landing/ returns 15+ matches across 6 files. AGENTS.md color scheme section states 'All other green/emerald usages must be migrated to orange/amber' but doesn't mention purple migration status.

summary: PASS. The landing page redesign is well-implemented. VideoBackground component properly handles fullscreen bgvid.mp4 with autoplay/loop/muted/playsInline, dark overlay for text legibility, and dark gradient fallback for slow connections. All required sections render correctly. CTA buttons route to correct paths (/login?mode=signup for signup, /home for dashboard). Auth-conditional header shows appropriate buttons based on login state. Card-based trust indicators section with generous spacing follows the requested aesthetic. Orange accent colors used consistently in section dividers and interactive elements. Responsive layout verified with mobile header, responsive text sizes, and flexible grid layouts. Four non-blocking issues noted: unnecessary AnimatePresence wrapper, opaque TestimonialsSection background, residual purple gradients in TestimonialCard, and minor export convention inconsistency.
